Details:
Data suggest that membrane separation is very effective in eliminating ibuprofen from water sources. A bench-scale study evaluating five types of NF and RO membranes under various hydrodynamic ratios and organic matter concentrations found ibuprofen removal rates ranging from 92 to 99 percent for four of the five membranes.
The other membrane, achieving 22 to 34 percent removal, had the highest molecular weight cutoff (MWCO) of any of the membranes at 400 Dalton. Poor removal by this particular membrane could possibly be attributed to the relatively low molecular weight of ibuprofen at 206 Dalton.
July 2009
